name: Silent Brainstorming
description: Participants generate ideas individually and in silence before sharing, reducing anchoring and groupthink.
background: A staple of facilitation and Liberating Structures.
why_it_exists: How do we hear every voice, not just the loudest?
what_good_looks_like: Everyone contributes before discussion; Ideas are anonymous where helpful; No debate during generation
common_pitfalls: Letting one person talk over the silence; Skipping straight to discussion, which favours extroverts
use_this_when: Starting a retrospective; Generating options in a workshop
Generic Use Case - When: At the start of an ideation activity
Generic Use Case - What: Each person writes ideas privately first
Generic Use Case - Summary: A quiet first round that surfaces a wider, more balanced set of ideas before any discussion.
Example / Use Case - What: A team brainstorming retro improvement ideas
Example / Use Case - Summary: Everyone wrote three ideas on cards in silence, then grouped them, producing twice the usual range of suggestions.
